original_tool_input.base_case: Over the next 30-60 days the rally remains technically intact (50d > 200d, price above both SMAs, VIX ~15) but is showing genuine topping stress: the 7-session ATH streak broke with a distribution-style gap-down/recovery day, sentiment has fallen 5 straight sessions, breadth is stuck in Fear, and credit spreads are holding at April-shock levels. Base case is a choppy, distribution-prone consolidation in the 7,500-7,620 zone with elevated risk of a corrective move toward the 23.6%/38.2% Fib re-entry zones (7,475 / 7,449) rather than a clean continuation to the ~7,679 upside target.
original_tool_input.trend_regime: 50-day SMA above 200-day SMA with price extended above both (+10.1% vs 50d, +14.6% vs 200d per report; analysis_context: +10.7% vs 200d). Bullish regime, but elevated/maturing extension with first momentum crack appearing.
original_tool_input.valuation_bucket: Forward PE ~21.4x (20x-22x band: cautious buying only at strong confluence); ERP ~0.19% sits in the 0.0-0.5% valuation-ceiling band = trim bias, no aggressive adds.

signals.bollinger_position: pulled away from upper band for first time after walking it
signals.middle_band_regime: above 20-day SMA, short-term structure still intact but weakening
signals.intraday_close_position: middle third (gap-down to 7,516 then recovery, failed to reclaim prior close)

what_changed_today: ATH streak BROKEN: first non-ATH close after 7 consecutive ATH sessions — single most important development vs prior runs.
Distribution-style session: gap-down open to ~7,516 (-93 pts), widest range of the stretch (~81 pts), recovered but failed to reclaim Jun 2 close — green close masking distribution.
RSI-14 cooled to ~62-65 at close, well below the 70+ readings that defined ATH days; 5-day RSI now shows a lower peak (momentum deterioration).
Triple bearish MFI divergence now visibly forming on the 3-month chart (mid-May through today).
Overall Fear & Greed fell a 5th consecutive session (57 -> 55), now -6 from the May 26 peak of 61 while price net rose.
Junk bond spread held at ~1.45% for a 3rd consecutive day — sustained credit warning, not a one-day anomaly.
Monte Carlo 7,700-vs-7,400 edge degraded toward the 65% threshold; analysis_context up-first probability ~62% does not meet the 70% Late Bull threshold (not actionable).
original_tool_input.narrative_summary: The Late Bull / Topping regime held but produced its first real crack: the 7-session ATH streak broke on a distribution-style gap-down-and-recover day, and every internal — falling sentiment (5th straight), flat Fear-zone breadth, ~1.45% credit spreads for a 3rd day, decelerating RSI, and a forming triple bearish MFI divergence — points the same direction while only VIX (~15) stays green. Valuation remains restrictive (ERP near zero, forward PE ~21.4x) and the Monte Carlo up-first edge (~62%) fails the 70% Late Bull threshold, so the simulation does not confirm action. The trend regime is still mechanically intact (50d > 200d, price above both), so the posture is disciplined hold-and-monitor with active trim planning rather than fresh defense. Watch for a close below ~7,400 or a 4th day of wide credit spreads to escalate from warning to action.
open_questions: Does the ATH at ~7,620 now act as confirmed resistance, or do bulls reclaim it on a clean close to revive the melt-up?
Will the junk-bond spread hold a 4th consecutive session at 1.45%+, moving credit from warning to alarm and forcing a VIX response?
Does the triple bearish MFI divergence resolve with a corrective break toward the 23.6%/38.2% Fib zone, or does breadth finally broaden out?
Will price tag the ~7,679 upside target before momentum fully exhausts, or has the cycle high already printed?
confirming_evidence: Trend regime mechanically intact: 50d > 200d and price above both SMAs.
VIX ~15 remains in a complacent/standard regime, below the 20 reliability threshold — no crisis signal and no volatility-spike capitulation.
Full-session recovery off the ~7,516 gap-down low shows dip buyers still present near support.
Monte Carlo still leans up-first (~62% raw) over the chosen horizon and median days favor the upside hit (9d up vs 14d down).

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Headline F&G still in Greed (55) and Market Momentum in Extreme Greed; price net higher over the week.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: F&G fell a 5th straight session (-6 from peak) while price rose — a classic falling-sentiment-near-highs topping divergence.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Falling sentiment during or near new highs is a topping warning.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: VIX ~15 and price above all SMAs signal calm, intact risk appetite.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Junk bond spread holding ~1.45% at April-shock levels for a 3rd day diverges from calm equities/VIX.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: When breadth and credit both diverge from price, elevate caution even if price has not yet broken down.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Index made fresh ATHs through Jun 2 and price holds above both SMAs.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: McClellan stuck ~1,050 in Fear and Stock Price Strength in Fear for a 9th session — index strength is narrow mega-cap leadership.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Headline strength with weak breadth indicates narrow leadership.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Price uptrend and earnings yield still positive; trend regime intact.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: ERP ~0.19% in the 0.0-0.5% valuation-ceiling band = trim bias, no aggressive adds, despite still-elevated price.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: ERP 0.0-0.5% is a valuation ceiling — trim bias, no aggressive adds.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: RSI recovered to ~62-65 intraday and price closed green.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: RSI lower peak on the 5-day view plus a forming triple bearish MFI divergence signals momentum deterioration as price holds near highs.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Bearish divergence: price makes a higher high while MFI/RSI makes a lower high; longer-timeframe divergences carry more weight.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Up-first probability (~62%) still favors upside and beats down-first.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: ~62% adjusted up-first fails the 70% Late Bull threshold across 65/70/75 — insufficient edge to act on the long.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: If adjusted probability does not meet the regime threshold, the setup is not actionable; never use Monte Carlo in isolation.

original_tool_input.primary_tension: A mechanically intact bull trend (50d > 200d, price above both SMAs, VIX ~15) is colliding with the first concrete topping crack — broken ATH streak, distribution-style session, decelerating momentum, and a unanimous internal fade (sentiment, breadth, credit) — while valuation sits at a ceiling and the Monte Carlo edge no longer clears the Late Bull threshold.
